lines: You're angry. [long pause] I'm not going to tell you not to be. [long pause] Anger is information. [long pause] It's telling you something matters. [long pause] But right now, anger is flooding your brain with cortisol, and that's shutting down the part of you that makes good decisions.

So we're not going to act. [long pause] Not yet. [long pause] We're going to buy time. [long pause] Twenty minutes — that's how long it takes for cortisol to clear. [long pause] This exercise gets you through the worst of it.

Your breath first. [long pause] We're going to breathe in for four counts and out for eight. [long pause] The long exhale activates your vagus nerve — it's the brake pedal for your nervous system. [long pause] It physically pulls you out of fight mode.
Breathe in... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Now out, slowly... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Out... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Out... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
In... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four.

Out... [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] eight.
Good. [long pause] Now let's find where the anger lives in your body. [long pause] Don't analyze it. [long pause] Just locate it.
Check your jaw. [long pause] Is it clenched? [long pause] Notice it. [long pause] Don't force it open. [long pause] Just notice.
Your fists. [long pause] Are they tight? [long pause] Just observe.
Your chest. [long pause] Is there pressure? [long pause] Heat?
Your stomach. [long pause] Tight? [long pause] Knotted?

Wherever you found it — direct your next exhale there. [long pause] Breathe in... [long pause] [long pause] and breathe out toward that spot. [long pause] Not to make it disappear. [long pause] Just to give it space.
One more time. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] and out, toward the tension.
Be quick to listen. [long pause] Slow to speak. [long pause] Slow to anger.
Your anger is real. [long pause] What someone did — or what happened — that's real. [long pause] But you get to choose what comes next. [long pause] Not the anger. [long pause] You.
There's a difference between feeling it and following it. [long pause] Right now, you've created that space. [long pause] Stay in it.
One more breath. [long pause] In through the nose... [long pause] [long pause] long exhale out.
You're in control. [long pause] Not because the anger is gone. [long pause] Because you chose not to let it drive.
